commit.message:
Fix all TLA+ verification gaps (G-1 through G-4) — 100% spec coverage (#37)

* feat: US-001 - Fix G-1: Use sorted composite key for BurnBuddy docs

- Accept handler now uses sorted composite key (${uid1}_${uid2}) as both
  the Firestore doc ID and the BurnBuddy.id field
- Checks for existing BurnBuddy before creating; returns 409 if duplicate
- Updated G-1 gap tests to assert correct behavior (composite key used,
  cross-request returns 409)
- Updated existing accept test to mock the new existence check

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-002] - Create requireProfile middleware and apply to burn-buddies routes

- Create requireProfile middleware that checks Firestore profile existence
- Add profile field to Express Request type augmentation
- Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /burn-buddies/requests
- Update G-4 gap tests to assert 403 when no profile exists
- Add test verifying profile-gated access works correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-003 - Apply requireProfile to friends, burn-squads, and workouts routes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-004 - Fix G-3: Username uniqueness race condition

Replace read-check-write pattern in generateUniqueUsername() with atomic
doc.create() calls. The function now atomically reserves usernames by
attempting to create the doc (which fails with ALREADY_EXISTS if taken),
eliminating the TOCTOU race condition where concurrent profile creations
could claim the same username.

- Changed generateUniqueUsername() to accept uid parameter and use
  doc.create() for atomic reservation
- Added tryReserveUsername() helper that handles ALREADY_EXISTS (code 6)
- Updated profile creation paths in users.ts to skip batch username
  reservation (now done in generateUniqueUsername)
- Added concurrent claim test in username.test.ts
- Updated G-3 gap tests to assert fixed behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-005] - Fix G-2: Explicit self-invite check in squad member add

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-006] - Update TLA+ verification report to 100% coverage

All four gaps (G-1 through G-4) are now marked as resolved with
descriptions of the fixes applied.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: mark US-006 complete in PRD and progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace ljmp with lretq for x86_64; suppress unused-function warnings

os/windows.c: 'ljmp $seg, $off' is unsupported by GAS in 64-bit mode.
Split the #ifdef: __i386__ keeps ljmp; __x86_64__ uses the lretq trick
(push CS=0, push RIP=VBR_LOAD_ADDR, lretq) which is the 64-bit
equivalent of a far jump. Also add (void)ep at function top to silence
unused-parameter warning.

os/linux.c, common/menu.c, bios/stage2/fat.c: mark static helper
functions (str_len, print_int, is_space) with __attribute__((unused))
to silence -Wunused-function without removing them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add point collider + speculative cone-twist limits

Point collider (PHYS_SHAPE_POINT / BONE_COLLIDER_POINT):
- Zero-volume collider for skeleton bones that need ground contact
  but have no authored collision geometry (replaces ghost body sinking)
- Generates single contact when body center penetrates halfspace
- Wired through: collider init, AABB update, serial narrowphase,
  parallel fused narrowphase, entity create, fskel loader, Blender export
- Bodies with point colliders get gravity and broadphase (unlike ghosts)

Speculative angular limits (cone-twist joint):
- Predict angle at end of timestep using relative angular velocity
- Activate constraint when predicted angle exceeds limit, even if
  current angle is within bounds
- Prevents single-frame angular overshoot for fast rotations

Also: enhanced p005 diagnostic output (bone names, 3D positions)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Make Cosmos DB optional during API startup

The API was crashing at startup when Cosmos was not available, preventing
standalone and test scenarios from running. This fix makes Cosmos registration
conditional based on configuration availability.

Changes:
- Made CosmosClient registration conditional (checks connection strings and config)
- Only register Cosmos-dependent services when Cosmos is configured
- Added user seeding during Cosmos initialization for identity
- Added CosmosUserService implementation for user authentication
- Log clear warning when running in standalone mode without persistence

This allows the API to start successfully in all environments:
- With Aspire: Full Cosmos initialization and seeding runs
- Without Aspire: API starts with warning, no data persistence
- Tests can now reach the API without requiring full orchestration

Fixes #44

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix contrast issues: primary chroma, accent surfaces, linked PR pills

- Light mode primary: oklch chroma 0.19→0.15 (less electric, easier on eyes)
- Light mode accent: oklch(87% 0.065 316) — clearly distinct from white card,
  Linkify/Add buttons now register as interactive
- Linked PR pills in MyIssues: opaque solid backgrounds
  (bg-purple-100/950 for merged, bg-emerald-100/950 for open)
  instead of bg-*/10 transparent which vanished on dark eggplant

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix camera frame relay: ffmpeg fallback, unicam filter, resilient push

- Add direct ffmpeg v4l2 capture as fallback when camera service is down
- Filter unicam driver from USB camera detection (CSI interface only)
- Remove failed cameras from manager so rescan loop retries
- Log camera service stderr for debugging
- Auto-create device_frames table if missing
- Add ffmpeg to bootstrap dependencies
- Better frame push logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: CMake compiler detection for cross toolchains; fix navbar logo overlap

cmake/toolchains: add CMAKE_TRY_COMPILE_TARGET_TYPE=STATIC_LIBRARY to
uefi-x86_64, uefi-aarch64 and bios toolchain files. CMake's compiler
detection tries to link a test binary which fails for freestanding
targets (no __main, no efi_main). STATIC_LIBRARY mode skips the link
step so detection succeeds.

docs/styles/classes.css: fix logo anchor tag overlapping 'Source' link
in the docs navbar. The .logo-link was position:absolute with left:80px
while the flex items started at padding-left:160px — the logo+text
spanned ~226px wide, overlapping 'Source' at 160px. Fixed by making the
logo-link a normal flex item instead of absolutely positioned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

48MP) from being processed at full resolution, fixing upload timeouts.

commit.message:
feat: replace Nominatim text geocoding with Azure OpenAI

New 3-step geocoding pipeline:
1. EXIF GPS  → Nominatim reverse geocode (kept — GPS is authoritative)
2. AOAI text → gpt-4o-mini extracts precise coordinates from message text
              (handles landmarks, restaurants, natural language context)
3. AOAI vision → gpt-4o-mini image recognition as last resort before giving up

Azure OpenAI resource: munchhatmap-aoai-prod (eastus, S0, Standard)
Model: gpt-4o-mini (10K TPM) — cheapest vision+text capable model

Changes:
- infra/modules/openai.bicep: new module, gpt-4o-mini Standard deployment
- infra/main.bicep: wire openai module, add openAiLocation param + output
- infra/modules/keyvault.bicep: aoai-endpoint and aoai-key placeholder secrets
- infra/modules/containerapp.bicep: AZURE_OPENAI_* env vars via KV secretRef
- bot/src/handlers/aoai.ts: geocodeWithText() and geocodeWithImage() via AzureOpenAI client
- bot/src/handlers/pinProcessor.ts: new 3-step flow, remove Nominatim text search
- bot/package.json: add openai ^6.27.0
- .env.example: document AZURE_OPENAI_* vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: hoist § markers from Lisp expressions and fix empty ASSIGN for collections (#550)

- Hoist §-containing operands in binary/unary operations to temp vars,
  preventing § section markers inside Lisp (op arg1 arg2) expressions
  that cause Calor0114 parse errors
- Fix empty §ASSIGN when RHS is a collection creation (List, Dict, Set,
  Array, MultiDimArray) by emitting the collection block with the
  assignment target name directly
- Add 8 new unit tests for hoisting and collection assignment patterns

Results on real-world codebases:
  Newtonsoft.Json: 239/239 = 100% (was 173/240 = 72.1%)
  Humanizer sample: 99/100 = 99% (was ~86.1%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix to prevent duplicate revision errors (#120)

* fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix to prevent duplicate revision errors

Add a data source to read existing container app state and compute whether
the revision suffix has actually changed. Only send the suffix via
azapi_update_resource when the user explicitly changes it, avoiding the
'revision with suffix already exists' error on updates.

- Add data.azapi_resource.existing to read current revision suffix
- Add locals for existing_revision_suffix and revision_suffix_to_send
- Add terraform_data.revision_suffix to track suffix changes
- Add azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix for conditional suffix updates
- Add examples/revision_suffix/ with test scenarios

Fixes #115

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix using data source + azapi_update_resource

Prevents 'revision with suffix already exists' error on subsequent applies
by separating revision suffix updates from the main resource PUT body.

Changes:
- Add data.azapi_resource.existing to read current state from Azure
- Add locals for existing_revision_suffix and revision_suffix_to_send
  (HasChange guard logic moved from locals.tf to main.tf)
- Add terraform_data.revision_suffix tracking var.template.revision_suffix
- Add azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix with replace_triggered_by
- Remove locals from locals.tf (now co-located with data source in main.tf)

* refactor: rename terraform_data and azapi_update_resource to more generic names

- terraform_data.revision_suffix -> terraform_data.update_keeper
- azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix -> azapi_update_resource.post_creation_update

* fix lint issue

* fix lint issue

* fix lint issue

* remove unused provider

* fix: use PATCH for template updates to prevent revision suffix and secrets errors

Main module changes:
- Extract template body into local.template_body to share between
  azapi_resource and azapi_resource_action
- Add ignore_changes for entire body.properties.template on the main
  azapi_resource to prevent PUT from re-sending revision-scoped properties
- Use azapi_resource_action with PATCH method instead of azapi_update_resource
  (which uses PUT and overwrites secrets) for post-creation template updates
- Track var.template changes via terraform_data.update_keeper to trigger
  replacement of azapi_resource_action.post_creation_update only when
  template properties actually change
- Use local.resource_group_id for data.azapi_resource.existing parent_id
  to avoid unnecessary data source reads when resource_group_id is provided

Example fixes:
- Add resource_group_id and location to all module calls across examples
  to ensure data.azapi_resource.existing can resolve at plan time
- Add subnet delegation for Microsoft.App/environments in vnet and acr examples
- Add ignore_changes for infrastructure_resource_group_name and
  workload_profile on container_app_environment in vnet and acr examples
- Split acr example into two subnets: one for private endpoints,
  one with delegation for container app environment

* chore(examples): register Microsoft.App resource provider via azapi_resource_action

Not all test subscriptions have Microsoft.App registered. Add
azapi_resource_action to register the provider in every example,
and ensure azurerm_container_app_environment depends_on it.

Also add azapi provider version constraint (~> 2.5) to examples
that were missing it.

* feat: add retry on azapi_resource_action for active provisioning conflict

* fix: use attribute syntax for retry block on azapi_resource_action

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Configure Vite port 5173 with proxy disabled for E2E tests

Root Cause Analysis (Issues #37, #38):
- AddViteApp creates an HTTP endpoint with DCP proxy enabled by default
- DCP proxy accepts connections on port 5173 but fails to forward to Vite
- Vite's HMR websockets are incompatible with Aspire's YARP proxy layer
- This caused: port 5173 listening but curl hangs, E2E tests blocked

Solution Applied:
- Configure endpoint with Port = 5173 and IsProxied = false
- Vite now binds directly to port 5173 (no proxy layer)
- HTTP/1.1 200 OK verified with curl test
- Matches documented workaround in dotnet/aspire#14470

Technical Details:
- Must use WithEndpoint("http") not WithHttpEndpoint() to modify existing endpoint
- IsProxied = false bypasses DCP proxy entirely
- Preserves service discovery via environment variables
- Fixes both manual curl tests and Playwright E2E test infrastructure

Closes #37 #38

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: AES transpose bug and stale test vectors

- Remove row/column transpose in aes_encrypt_block and aes_decrypt_block;
  AES state is stored column-major so load/store is a straight memcpy
- Drop aes_init_dec inv_mix_col transformation: direct inverse cipher
  requires the forward key schedule, not the equivalent-inverse schedule
- Remove unused inv_mix_col helper
- Correct HMAC-SHA256 RFC 4231 TC2 expected value (was wrong in test;
  implementation was always correct per openssl verification)
- Correct PBKDF2-SHA256 BasicVector expected value (was wrong in test;
  implementation matches hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac reference)

All 56 tests now pass (19 config + 19 crypto + 18 luks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor PresentPixmap to use struct-based options

Refactored PresentPixmap from 11 positional parameters to a single
PixmapPresentOptions struct with well-documented fields. This improves:

- Call-site readability: No more confusion about parameter order
- Future extensibility: Easy to add new optional fields
- Type safety: Named fields prevent parameter swapping errors

Changes:
- Added PixmapPresentOptions struct with 13 documented fields
- Updated PresentPixmap signature to accept single options parameter
- Updated all call sites (3 locations):
  - cmd/x11-dmabuf-demo/main.go
  - cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go
  - internal/x11/present/present_test.go (2 test cases)
- Simplified call sites using zero values for unused fields

Tests: All tests pass (make test-go)
Build: Verified with make build

Resolves AUDIT.md MEDIUM priority finding.

repository.description: A modern, enterprise-grade step tracking web application designed specifically for the CxE Americas Offsite 2025. Features real-time weather integration, dark mode, offline support, and comprehensive team competition.

commit.message:
fix: Vite frontend now binds to port 5173 for Aspire orchestration

- Updated start script to explicitly set --port 5173
- Fixes E2E infrastructure blockers #37 #38
- Frontend now responds on localhost:5173 with HTTP 200
- Enables E2E test suite to reach application

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add static/demo mode for GitHub Pages with mock data and cross-page navigation

- index.html: Add STATIC_MODE detection, comprehensive mock product data (32 products),
  and getMockData() function that supports filtering/pagination for /api/products
- index.html: Replace api() to return mock data when not on localhost
- index.html: Add sidebar links to docs.html, marketing.html, and GitHub repo
- index.html: Show 'Demo Mode' badge in static mode
- index.html: Guard exports, schedule CRUD, and WebSocket behind STATIC_MODE
- docs.html: Fix nav logo to link to marketing.html, add Dashboard and GitHub links,
  fix Live Dashboard CTA to link to index.html
- marketing.html: Fix LICENSE links from blob/main to blob/master

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
